info.name: AppMax — Full Integration Suite
info.description: Combined collection covering the full Appmax integration. Two top-level folders: 'Appmax Endpoints' calls the Appmax sandbox APIs directly; 'Localhost Endpoints' calls the backend server at localhost:8080.

item.name: Appmax Endpoints
Installation Flow

request.description: Gets the app Bearer token from Appmax Keycloak. Also generates a unique EXTERNAL_KEY for this installation attempt.
Generates the installation hash. Check the Postman console for the browser URL.

exec:
1. Log in with AppMax sandbox credentials
  2. Fill store name, select company, check the checkbox
  3. Click Salvar
  4. Wait for redirect — then continue to Step 4
After authorizing, AppMax will automatically POST the health check to your callback URL. Step 5 simulates this manually if needed.

request.description:
Displays the browser authorization URL in the Visualize tab. Click the link, log in as merchant, and authorize.

After clicking Salvar, AppMax will redirect to your callback and also POST the health check automatically.

exec: console.error('504 Gateway Timeout — AppMax Keycloak issue. CF-Ray:', cfRay);
pm.test('504 — hash may be retried or escalate to AppMax support', () => pm.expect(true).to.be.true);

request.description:
Fetches merchant client_id and client_secret from AppMax.

**Note:** The sandbox Keycloak may return 504. If so, note the CF-Ray header and report to AppMax support. AppMax also sends these credentials automatically via the health check POST.

item.name: Step 5 — Health Check POST (simulate AppMax callback)

request.description:
Simulates the server-to-server POST that AppMax sends automatically after merchant authorization.

**Note:** `app_id` uses the numeric ID (889), not the UUID — this is what AppMax sends in production.

Expected response: `{"external_id": "<uuid>"}`
Exchanges merchant credentials for a JWT Bearer token (valid 3600s). Validates that the credentials work.

item.description: Step-by-step App installation flow. Run steps 1–6 in order to obtain a merchant token. Step 3 must be completed in a browser.

request.description: Creates or updates a customer. Captures CUSTOMER_ID for subsequent requests.
Creates a new order for R$100,00 (10000 cents). Captures ORDER_ID.
Processes a credit card payment using the sandbox success card (4000000000000010). Captures UPSELL_HASH and APPROVED_ORDER_ID.
Creates a fresh order for the declined card test. Captures ORDER_ID_DECLINED.
Uses the sandbox decline card (4000000000000028). Expected: error response from Appmax.
Creates a fresh order for the PIX payment test. Captures ORDER_ID_PIX.
Generates a PIX QR code and EMV string. pix_qrcode is base64-encoded PNG.
Creates a fresh order for the boleto payment test. Captures ORDER_ID_BOLETO.
Generates a boleto with PDF link and digitable line.
Retrieves full order details (status, payment, customer) for the approved credit card order.

Approves the order before requesting partial refund.

exec: } else { console.warn('Partial refund blocked (sandbox balance):', JSON.stringify(json)); }

request.description: Partial refund of R$50,00 (5000 cents). Note: may return 400 in sandbox without merchant balance.
Registers a shipping tracking code on the approved order. Required for merchant withdrawals.
Creates an upsell order using the upsell_hash from the approved credit card payment (step 3a).
Fresh order for recurring credit card test.
Credit card payment with subscription object (interval=month, interval_count=1). Requires merchant to have the recurrence app installed in Appmax.
Fresh order for recurring PIX test.
PIX payment with subscription object. Requires merchant to have the recurrence app installed.
Registers an abandoned cart with cart_link for AI-powered sales recovery. Beta feature — requires activation by Appmax.

exec: // Apple Pay cannot be tested in sandbox without a real Apple device
// This request is kept as API contract documentation
console.warn('Apple Pay requires real Apple device. This is a placeholder request.');

request.description:
Apple Pay payment endpoint. Sends the encrypted appleToken generated by Apple's PaymentSheet to Appmax.

IMPORTANT: Cannot be executed in Postman directly — the appleToken must be generated by a real Apple device via Safari. This request documents the API contract only.

--- HOW TO TEST ---

Requirements:
- iPhone, iPad, or Mac with Touch ID
- Safari browser (Apple Pay JS only works in Safari)
- Apple Wallet configured with a test card (sandbox Apple ID)
- Your checkout page served over HTTPS with Apple Pay domain verification

Step 1 — Set up a sandbox Apple ID:
  Go to Settings > App Store > Sandbox Account on your iPhone/iPad
  or System Settings > App Store > Sandbox on Mac.
  Create or use an existing sandbox Apple ID.

Step 2 — Add a test card to Apple Wallet:
  Apple Pay test cards (add via Wallet app on sandbox account):
  - Visa:       4761 1200 1000 0492  exp 11/2022  CVV 533
  - Mastercard: 5204 2477 5000 1471  exp 11/2022  CVV 111
  - Amex:       3714 4963 5398 431   exp 11/2022  CVV 1234

Step 3 — Open your checkout page in Safari on the device:
  The page must be served over HTTPS (use the ngrok URL).
  Apple Pay button will only appear if the domain is verified and the device has a card.

Step 4 — Complete the Apple Pay flow:
  Tap the Apple Pay button → authenticate with Face ID / Touch ID.
  The device generates an encrypted PKPaymentToken and your frontend sends it
  as the appleToken field in the request body below.

Step 5 — Inspect the token (optional):
  Check ngrok request logs or your app logs to capture the exact appleToken
  structure for future reference.

item.description:
Full payment flow: customer → order → payment methods → status → refund.

Run sequentially. Variables are chained automatically between requests.

Pre-requisite: MERCHANT_TOKEN must be set (run Step 6 first or set manually).

item.name: Payment Splits

request.description: Creates a recipient for payment split. Captures RECIPIENT_HASH needed for all subsequent split operations.
Sends an SMS to the recipient's phone with a facial recognition link to complete KYC. Required before the recipient can receive split payments.
Checks the current onboarding/KYC status of the recipient. Expected values: 'Awaiting face match completion' → 'Onboarding on verification' → 'Onboarding completed'.

exec: pm.test('Split created', () => pm.expect(json.message).to.eql('Split order created successfully'));

request.description: Distributes R$30,00 (3000 cents) of the approved order's net value to the registered recipient. Requires the order to be approved and the recipient to have completed onboarding.
Returns available and to_release balances for the recipient.

exec: console.log('Gross:', json.data.value, '| Net:', json.data.net_value, '| Tax%:', json.data.tax_percentage);

request.description: Simulates anticipation withdrawal of R$100,00. Returns net value after fees. No withdrawal is created.
Requests withdrawal of R$100,00 from available balance. Returns withdraw_request_id.

exec: console.log('Anticipation:', json.data.value, '→ Net:', json.data.net_value, '| Tax:', json.data.withdraw_tax);

request.description: Requests anticipation withdrawal of R$100,00. Deducts anticipation fee from net value.

item.description:
Full split payment flow: create recipient → KYC → check status → split order → check balances → simulate anticipation → withdraw.

NOTE: These endpoints exist only in production (api.appmax.com.br). The sandbox (api.sandboxappmax.com.br) does not route /v1/recipient. All requests return "Client id not found" in production when using sandbox credentials — this is expected. To execute: use production merchant credentials obtained via a live installation.

item.name: Webhook Simulations
item.description: Simulates all 26 Appmax webhook events against the local app endpoint. Run AFTER creating an order so APPROVED_ORDER_ID is set.
All requests targeting the Appmax sandbox APIs directly (auth.sandboxappmax.com.br / api.sandboxappmax.com.br).

item.name: Validations

exec: pm.test('Status 200 — invalid token gracefully ignored', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description: If the token is not in cache (expired or non-existent), returns 200 graceful.

item.description:
These requests intentionally send invalid input to verify the API rejects them correctly. Each non-200 response is expected and confirms the validation is working:

• Start without app_id → 400 (app_id is required)
• Callback GET with invalid token → 200 graceful (expired/unknown token is ignored silently)
• Callback POST with wrong app_id → 400 (app_id must match the registered app)

item.description: Demonstrates the declined card scenario. A tokenized card with number 4000000000000028 is always declined by the Appmax sandbox. The Pay endpoint returns 422 with message 'payment declined' — this is expected and correct.

exec: // Sandbox: orders are not yet settled so Appmax returns 404.

item.description:
Order Status for a non-existent order ID (999999) returns 404 — this confirms not-found handling works correctly.

Note: Refund requests in Happy Path may return 404 in sandbox because Appmax requires orders to be settled before refunding. In production, refunds on paid and settled orders return 200.
